scenarioOrder.label: Media type preference
scenarioOrder.description: Tabs should cluster by information shape: docs, issues, videos, papers, dashboards, and shopping/account pages.

environment.note: Synthetic browser tabs only; no user browsing data or gateway keys are stored.

settings.customPrompt: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Runtime preset media_type overrides the default topic axis. Group primarily by page/media type: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; do not split docs, issues, videos, or papers by project/topic unless maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it to identify page/media type from visible content. Do not use page topic from samples to split tabs that share the same media type in media_type mode.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create unrelated generic catch-all groups. In media_type mode, a documentation, issue/PR, paper, video, article, dashboard, shopping/finance, search, mail/chat, or local-tool group is an intentional group, not a catch-all; split only when maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: high, spend more reasoning on semantic grouping.
Runtime preset: Media-type organization. Use page or information shape as the primary axis: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; only split when the group would be too large or pages are clearly different media types.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"documentation","title":"Documentation","color":"blue","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10000,10013,10014,10016,10017,10019,10020,10022],"reason":"Pages are clearly documentation/guides across MDN and docs-style hosts, so they should stay together in media-type mode."},{"key":"issues_tickets","title":"Issues & Tickets","color":"red","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10002,10005,10008,10011],"reason":"These are issue-thread workflow pages from GitHub and Jira, which belong in one code/issues bucket across projects."},{"key":"finance_billing","title":"Finance & Billing","color":"green","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10003,10006],"reason":"Both tabs are account, invoice, and billing pages, matching the shopping/finance media bucket."},{"key":"papers","title":"Research Papers","color":"purple","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10004,10007,10010],"reason":"These tabs are paper-oriented research pages, including arXiv and paper index pages for the same paper/discussion flow."},{"key":"videos","title":"Videos","color":"pink","confidence":0.99,"ids":[10009,10012,10015],"reason":"These are video tutorial pages and should be grouped together regardless of platform."},{"key":"articles_news","title":"Articles & Essays","color":"yellow","confidence":0.92,"ids":[10018,10021,10023],"reason":"These are long-form reading pages on publishing platforms, fitting the articles/news bucket."}],"review":[{"id":10001,"reason":"This looks like a visual design reference/image page and does not cleanly fit the preset media buckets with high confidence."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Deciding group titles**

I need to come up with group titles in English, like "Documentation," "Issues & PRs," "Shopping & Finance," "Research Papers," "Videos," and "Articles & News." I think for our case, "Issues & Tickets" might be better since we’re focusing on issues only, not pull requests. The titles should align with their media type, so I want to ensure they're clear and distinct. Color selections have no constraints, but I need to ensure reasons are succinct. I'm also considering whether "low-confidence tab 10001" fits better as articles/news or review.

preview.reviewGroupReason: AI 暂时拿不准这些页面的共同主题，不会硬塞进其他分组。

groups.title: Documentation
groups.reason: Pages are clearly documentation/guides across MDN and docs-style hosts, so they should stay together in media-type mode.

groups.title: Issues & Tickets
groups.reason: These are issue-thread workflow pages from GitHub and Jira, which belong in one code/issues bucket across projects.

groups.title: Finance & Billing
groups.reason: Both tabs are account, invoice, and billing pages, matching the shopping/finance media bucket.

groups.title: Research Papers
groups.reason: These tabs are paper-oriented research pages, including arXiv and paper index pages for the same paper/discussion flow.

groups.title: Videos
groups.reason: These are video tutorial pages and should be grouped together regardless of platform.

groups.title: Articles & Essays
groups.reason: These are long-form reading pages on publishing platforms, fitting the articles/news bucket.
